source: v4.1 Part G — dairy commodity calibration notes
scope: Applies to dairy-uk-eu commodity (8322 Cat 3 ABP and 8468 human consumption)

entries.title: I.5 = I.6 SAME-ENTITY PATTERN (H2 NOTE AUTHORITY)
entries.description: Where an H2 consignee library entry carries the note `I.5 and I.6 same entity = normal`, the same-entity dual-role pattern is confirmed standing practice on that consignee's lanes — silent pass on I.5 = I.6. Novades BV and Farmel Dairy Products BV are the current confirmed entries; new entries follow as H2 is extended (no rule-set amendment required to add a new same-entity consignee). Do not flag on H2 note presence. Where the I.5 = I.6 pattern appears for an H2 entry WITHOUT this note, fall through to B1 I.6 (`Same entity as I.5 = normal on some lanes — do not flag`) — still silent for established lanes, BLUE low notice only on genuinely first-time appearance per the engine's universal new-entity rule.

entries.title: AFI I.5 CONSIGNEE PATTERN
entries.description: Standard rule: Arla Foods Ingredients Group P/S (Viby-J, Denmark) as I.5 consignee on all standard AFI EHCs is confirmed standing practice regardless of I.9 destination country and regardless of I.12 end customer identity. Never flag. EXCEPTION (AMENDED v4.0) — Irish and Northern Irish destinations: a named consignee appears in I.5 (e.g. Gobia Ltd, Dairy Consumer Foods, J M W Farms). This is correct and expected on Irish/NI lanes. Arla Foods Ingredients Group P/S in I.5 on an Irish or NI-destined EHC = medium warning — OV to confirm with exporter.

entries.title: SAPUTO / DAIRY CREST BATCH TRUNCATION
entries.description: AQ-prefix batch numbers presented as root only (e.g. 'AQ6070', 'AQ6099') on Davidstow/Saputo (GB CQ 501) loads is confirmed standard practice. Generate NO flag of any kind — not even a blue notice. Silent pass at all severity levels. This does NOT apply to AFI/Arla (GB DE 030) loads, where full AF-prefix batch numbers are required. Truncated root on AFI loads = hard error. AMENDED v4.1: on Fines and downgrade loads from Davidstow/Saputo (GB CQ 501), full AQ-prefix batch references including F-suffix run number (e.g. 'AQ6079F03') appear on the schedule page — correct and expected for this product type. Silent pass.

entries.title: VAN BENTUM TRAILERS
entries.description: BTS-prefix trailer numbers (e.g. BTS2295, BTS2359) are Van Bentum (Netherlands) trailers, the expected haulier on Zeebrugge/BE-BEZEE1 ro-ro vessel loads for Novades BV. Dutch trailers — foreign plates are expected and normal.

entries.title: AFI VARIOLAC WHEY PERMEATE
entries.description: DAIRY WHEY POWDER and WHEY PERMEATE POWDER in I.27/I.28 Nature of commodity on AFI Variolac whey permeate loads at Calais FRCQF1 and Esbjerg DKEBJ1 are both confirmed standard practice variants. Generate NO flag of any kind — not even a blue notice. Silent pass at all severity levels. The actual commodity is whey permeate powder; both 'DAIRY WHEY POWDER' and 'WHEY PERMEATE POWDER' are accepted descriptions on these lanes. AMENDED v3.5: blue notice removed entirely (was previously low notice). AMENDED v3.9: WHEY PERMEATE POWDER added as accepted silent-pass variant.

entries.title: 8322 SCHEDULE PAGES
entries.description: EHC 8322 may include one or more schedule pages appended after the standard 10 pages (or 5 pages for English-only versions), giving a total page count of 11 of 11, 12 of 12, etc. This is correct and expected — do not flag additional pages on 8322 as a pagination error. Schedule pages must carry the EHC reference, be signed, stamped and dated by the OV, and be sequentially numbered as part of the complete document.

entries.title: HEATHFIELD (CAT 3 ABP STORAGE): APPROVAL NUMBER PENDING CONFIRMATION
entries.description: There is a Cat 3 ABP storage approval number for the Heathfield depot (Gregory's Distribution Ltd). This applies specifically where I.11 Place of dispatch is the Heathfield address on a Cat 3 (8322) load — i.e. the load is dispatched from Heathfield cold store rather than directly from the manufacturing site at Davidstow. Where I.11 shows the Davidstow manufacturing address, the Heathfield approval is not applicable. Current status: approval number not yet confirmed. Where an 8322 EHC is submitted with Heathfield as I.11 on a Cat 3 load, apply a medium warning and request the OV to supply the Cat 3 storage approval number for library entry. This note will be updated to a confirmed standing pass once the number is verified and added to H1.

entries.title: 8468 FR SIGNING PAGE REFERENCE: SCAN COMPRESSION ARTEFACT
entries.description: On scanned 8468 bilingual certificates, the II.a reference on the French signing page (page 11) may render with the leading characters of the reference compressed or partially obscured, causing digits such as "26" to read as "20" or similar. Do not flag a reference mismatch on this page based on scan appearance alone where all other pages are consistent and the physical document reads correctly.

entries.title: AFI/ARLA COMMERCIAL DOCUMENT REFERENCE: INVOICE PO MISMATCH
entries.description: The I.17 commercial document reference on AFI Taw Valley powder/curtainsider EHCs is a load-level dispatch reference generated by Gregory Distribution, matching the ORDER field on Gregory's dispatch note and the EHC filename. AFI/Arla invoices carry separate internal Arla purchase order numbers which will routinely differ. Mismatch between I.17 and the AFI invoice = silent pass. Cross-verification of I.17 is satisfied by Gregory dispatch note and filename match. Does NOT apply to AFI bulk tanker LPC loads — on those, the WPC product batch reference is used as I.17 documentary reference and will match the DN directly. Source: v4.1 calibration (resolves false positive on cert 26/2/120225).

entries.title: AFI LPC TANKER TO DENMARK: M119 AT I.12
entries.description: M119 in I.12 Approval No on AFI LPC tanker EHCs to Denmark (Arla Foods Ingredients Group P/S Danmark Protein, Videbaek) is a standing requirement — hard error if absent. Its presence is correct and expected. No flag of any kind — not even a blue notice. Source: v4.1 calibration.
